summary:
WEEKLY REVIEW — 2026-06-20

All four status reads succeeded. UFC is the only project actively producing output; crypto and volleyball are at zero activity with no errors (indistinguishable from healthy-idle, see enhancements).

ISSUES FOUND
- [overseer #3] Malformed repo slug: the volleyball status source resolves to "AndyRBrett/volleyball\r\n/overseer-status.json" — a stray CRLF in the configured slug. Reads tolerate it, but any GitHub API call reusing the slug (file_issue, links) will 404. Fix: strip + regex-validate slugs at config load; add regression test. (Dedup check ran clean.)

No other confirmed bugs: crypto/volleyball zero-activity weeks reported no errors, and UFC is clean (49 runs/7d, 100% success, data fresh).

ENHANCEMENT IDEAS
- Crypto [#18, low/high]: trades=0, pnl=0, last_fill=null, errors=[]. Add last_run_at + signals_evaluated counters so "no signal" is distinguishable from "pipeline dead." Top priority — silent-failure blind spot on a bot trading real money.
- Volleyball [#4, low/med]: 0 footage / 0 frames / 0 errors looks identical whether idle or broken. Add last_footage_at + expected-vs-actual frame counts to catch "footage uploaded but 0 frames out."
- UFC [#12, med/med]: healthy. Replace single global odds_fingerprint + 24h threshold with per-event freshness timestamps and persisted odds snapshots, enabling open-to-current line-movement charts (the core value of an odds tracker) and catching one stale event the global hash hides.
- Overseer [#4, low/high]: SELF-CRITICAL — overseer treats errors=[] as green, so crypto & volleyball both render healthy despite producing nothing all week. Add a YELLOW "blind/idle" state (no errors + no activity + stale last_*_at) and surface it in the digest. Add status-schema unit tests (none exist; would have caught the CRLF-slug bug). Also: only 2 overseer runs in 7d — verify the weekly schedule is firing and isn't itself partially blind.

Theme this week: the monitor and two of three projects can't currently tell "quiet" from "dead." The low-effort/high-impact wins (crypto #18, overseer #4) both close that gap and should go first.
